rule_text: Price preference for AP Government stores procurement is delegated to Director CSP: 10% for indigenous products over imported; 5% for local AP-State products over other Indian products; 10% for Cottage and Small Scale Industries; 5% for Cooperative Societies; reservations for SSI must be adhered to.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 123 / Delegation I(1)(i)-(v)
verification_method: Check tender BDS specifies price-preference matrix matching the 10/5/10/5 scheme; reject if absent or wrongly weighted

rule_text: Director CSP may allow advance payment up to 90% of contract value on receipt of Railway Receipts in cases of REPUTED FIRMS, with balance on arrival + inspection — provided this is stipulated in the tender agreement.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 123 / Delegation I(2)
verification_method: Check tender SCC: if 90% RR-backed advance allowed, verify reputed-firm classification on file + agreement clause stipulating this

rule_text: AP Stores tender financial-acceptance ceilings: Director CSP may accept tenders up to Rs.2 lakh; Assistant Directors up to Rs.25,000 — without referring to higher authorities; cases where lowest-vs-recommended tender difference exceeds 10% OR Rs.20,000 must be referred to High Power Committee.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 123 / Delegation I(3)
verification_method: Check tender Acceptance Letter signatory rank vs ECV: <= Rs.25,000 → AD; Rs.25,000-Rs.2 lakh → Director; > Rs.2 lakh OR L1-vs-recommended-difference > 10%/Rs.20,000 → HPC referral

rule_text: All AP rate contracts entered into for more than ONE YEAR must be approved by the High Power Committee before being operated by Director CSP.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 123 / Delegation I(4)
verification_method: Check rate-contract file: HPC approval order on record; reject multi-year rate contracts without HPC sanction

rule_text: AP CSP Security Deposit slabs: 10% of contract value for indents EXCEEDING Rs.10,000 (after agreement-signing in name of Governor); flat Rs.100 for orders Rs.2,000-Rs.5,000; flat Rs.200 for Rs.5,000-Rs.10,000; SSI / Government Institutions / work centres / Federation of Industrial Co-ops / Ordnance Factories / other-State and Central Government Institutions are EXEMPT on reciprocal basis.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 123 / Delegation I(5) and N.B.
verification_method: Check Security Deposit on file matches the slab; verify SSI/Govt-institution exemption status if SD waived

rule_text: AP CSP tender enquiry methods: 'Limited Tender' system is the rule for demands up to Rs.5,000 OR where it is known that very few firms deal in the required stores (addressed only to firms on the CSP approved list); 'Single Tender' system is used to procure proprietary articles with sole agency.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 123 / Delegation I(7)-(8)
verification_method: For Limited Tender: ECV <= Rs.5,000 OR market-narrowness justification on file; for Single Tender: proprietary-sole-agency certificate on file

rule_text: Repeat orders by Director CSP allowed only if (a) no downward price trend in last 6 months from original order, AND (b) repeat quantity does NOT exceed original for orders <= Rs.50,000, OR 50% of original (or Rs.50,000 whichever more) for larger orders; deviations require Director's written reasons + HPC sanction.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 123 / Delegation I(9)
verification_method: Repeat order requires: original order date < 6 months prior + price-non-decline certification + qty within slab limits OR HPC sanction

rule_text: AP CSP delivery-period extensions delegated: Director CSP may extend up to 6 months; Assistant Directors up to 3 months provided purchase value <= Rs.25,000; longer extensions require Stores Purchase Committee approval; extensions should be 'rare and exceptional', not granted as a matter of rule.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 123 / Delegation II(2)
verification_method: Check extension order signatory rank matches: AD up to 3mo + value <= Rs.25,000; Director up to 6mo; SPC approval for >6mo

rule_text: Director CSP may pass over the lowest tender ONLY for these recorded reasons: (a) stores not meeting specifications, (b) delivery period unsuitable, (c) supplier's previous performance unsatisfactory, (d) inspection report showing unsatisfactory capacity/technical-equipment/financial-position; written reasons must be on file.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 123 / Delegation II(3)
verification_method: If L1 bypassed: check written reasons on file matching one of the 4 grounds (a-d); reject lowest-bypass without recorded justification

rule_text: Emergency direct purchase by AP Heads of Departments is allowed to UNLIMITED extent, but ONLY with prior approval of Stores Purchase Committee OR Government in concerned Secretariat Department in consultation with Industries Department, AND subject to budget revision.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 123 / Delegation II(4)
verification_method: For emergency purchases: SPC OR Secretariat-Industries-Dept approval order on file; budget-revision-recorded note
